It belongs to the genus Calceolaria, which includes flowering plants commonly known as pocket books or slipper flowers. The species Calcearia taliensis is classified as a perennial plant, meaning it lives for more than two years. Calcearia taliensis is a rare and endangered species, found primarily in the mountainous regions of China.
